method.description: Coverage census of IMF CPIS/PIP bilateral portfolio-investment positions. Each candidate ISO-3 economy in CL_PIP_COUNTRY(2.0.0) was probed as a REPORTER with one compact serieskeysonly query (total PI assets, holder sector S1, issuer sector S1, all counterparts, annual, startPeriod=2024), counting distinct COUNTERPART_COUNTRY series that resolve. A 14-economy load-bearing reporter x counterpart sub-matrix was then probed cell-by-cell (P_TOTINV_P_USD, S1/S1, semi-annual, startPeriod=2025). All HTTP 200.

reporters_enumerated.note: CL_PIP_COUNTRY(2.0.0) has 347 total members; 259 are pure 3-alpha (potential economies), 88 are special aggregates (GX*, TX*, W*, _T etc.). The 259 are the candidate universe for both reporter and counterpart positions.

counterparts_enumerated.note: Same codelist serves COUNTERPART_COUNTRY (pos 5). Major reporters resolve up to 244-245 distinct counterparts.

active_reporters.definition: Economies returning >0 bilateral counterpart series as a CPIS reporter for total PI assets, S1/S1, annual, 2024+.
active_reporters.non_reporting_note: These 176 ISO-3 codes are VALID codelist members and resolve as COUNTERPARTS, but the economies do not FILE CPIS as reporters. Their emptiness as reporters is a GENUINE structural feature of CPIS (it is a voluntary reporter survey), confirmed against valid codes -> not a code bug.

density_among_reporters.interpretation: For the reporting subset the from-whom-to-whom row is dense: a typical reporter resolves ~244 of 259 candidate counterparts. The matrix is sparse only in the REPORTER dimension (83 of 259 file), not in the counterpart dimension.

load_bearing_cell_matrix.code_correction: Cayman Islands code is CYM (verified in CL_PIP_COUNTRY), NOT 'KYM' as the probe brief guessed. KYM is ABSENT from the codelist. This is exactly the from-memory code error the audit warned about; corrected from the live codelist.

VGB.code_validity: VGB is a VALID codelist member and resolves as a COUNTERPART (e.g. USA->VGB 2025-S1 = 51,419,000,000 USD), but British Virgin Islands files NO CPIS data as a reporter. Confirmed: empty dataset, HTTP 200, 1304 bytes, code valid -> genuine hole in the reporter row.

as_counterpart.note: All 14 load-bearing economies resolve as COUNTERPARTS for reporter USA, semi-annual, 2025-S1 (HTTP 200, real obs). USA->USA is empty (no self-holdings cell), which is expected and not a hole.

load_bearing_cell_matrix.submatrix_density: Of the 14x14 load-bearing reporter x counterpart grid: every (reporter,counterpart) cell with reporter in {USA,DEU,FRA,ITA,NLD,JPN,GBR,CHN,CYM,HKG,BEL,LUX,IRL} and counterpart != reporter populates with real obs (verified live). VGB reporter row is entirely empty (genuine hole). Diagonal self-cells are empty by construction.

genuine_holes.hole: VGB (British Virgin Islands) as a REPORTER
genuine_holes.detail: Does not file CPIS. 0 counterpart series. Code VGB confirmed valid (resolves as counterpart). Structural: BVI is not a CPIS reporting economy.

genuine_holes.hole: 176 of 259 candidate ISO-3 economies do not report bilaterally to CPIS
genuine_holes.detail: Genuine non-reporters (CPIS is a voluntary creditor survey). Codes valid as counterparts. This is the inherent reporter-side sparsity of CPIS, not a query defect.

genuine_holes.hole: HKG -> {BEL, ITA} (and similar small counterparts) have NO recent vintage
genuine_holes.detail: Codes valid; data EXISTS but Hong Kong's series for these specific small counterparts STOPS at 2021 (last obs 2021/2021-S2). The 2024+/2025 probe correctly returned empty. This is a VINTAGE-STALENESS friction for selected reporter-counterpart cells, not a missing code and not a structural hole for the cell as such. Reporters can drop/stop individual bilateral cells across vintages.

resolved_suspects.resolution: Transient curl failure in the parallel probe, NOT an empty dataset. Re-probed single-threaded: HTTP 200, 2025-S1 = 103,064,508,000 USD. NOT a hole.
Re-probed freq-wild since 2018: data exists (annual + semi-annual) but stops at 2021. Reclassified as stale_cell, see genuine_holes.

density_summary: HONEST DENSITY: 83 of 259 candidate ISO-3 economies report bilaterally to CPIS as creditors; the typical reporter resolves ~244 of 259 counterparts (median 244, 51 reporters at >=200). The from-whom-to-whom matrix is DENSE in the counterpart dimension and dense among the 83 reporters, but SPARSE in the reporter dimension (176 non-reporters, valid as counterparts only). All 14 load-bearing economies populate as counterparts; 13 of 14 also populate as reporters. VGB is the one load-bearing economy that does NOT report (genuine hole). HKG reports but has stale/dropped small-counterpart cells (e.g. BEL, ITA frozen at 2021). The earlier DP2 'one-dense-column-only / HOLE' verdict is refuted: the matrix is genuinely dense for the reporting subset; the apparent emptiness was the US-vs-USA code bug (HTTP-200-empty trap).
